criteria.description: Matching entries print one per line in human-readable format
Results stream to stdout as produced, not batched
Errors and warnings go to stderr only
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error, 2 on usage error

AC_1.evidence: FormatText produces single-line human-readable output with [TIMESTAMP] LEVEL: MESSAGE key=value format. 3 unit tests (TestOU1_FormatTextFullEntry, TestOU1_FormatTextMissingFields, TestOU1_FormatTextOneLine) verify format, missing field graceful handling, and no embedded newlines. Integration test TestOU1_SearchPrintsMatchingEntries confirms 3 entries produce 3 stdout lines.

AC_2.evidence: StreamEntry writes each entry immediately via fmt.Fprintln to the io.Writer. TestNF5_StreamWritesImmediately verifies buffer has content after first entry before second is written. search.go loop calls StreamEntry per entry. Output is not accumulated into a buffer.

AC_3.evidence: All error/warning messages in search.go use fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...). ParseNDJSONWithWarnings receives os.Stderr for malformed-line warnings. TestNF4_SearchErrorsToStderr verifies stdout contains no 'warning' text and stderr does contain it, using separate stdout/stderr buffers on actual binary execution.

AC_4.evidence: runSearch returns 0 (success), 1 (file/parse error), 2 (missing argument). main() passes return value to os.Exit(). Three integration tests verify each code: TestNF3_SearchExitCodeSuccess (exit 0), TestNF3_SearchExitCodeError (exit 1 for nonexistent file), TestNF3_SearchExitCodeUsage (exit 2 for missing arg).

verificationReports.notes: Pre-flight: build clean, 64 tests pass across 5 packages, go vet clean. Minor: gofmt reports internal/filter/filter.go needs formatting but that is not part of this iteration. All tests are non-tautological -- they test real behavior against known inputs/outputs using actual binary execution for integration tests.
